title: Uplifted Syrian Bread Flatbread
description: A yeasty, pillowy flatbread studded with raisins and finished with a touch of egg, perfect for a show‑off Syrian lunch. This ovo‑vegetarian dish balances sweet and savory for a comforting bite.
instructions: In a small bowl, mix warm water, warm milk, and sugar, then add yeast and let sit for 5 minutes until frothy.
In a large mixing bowl, sift flour and salt together, then create a well in the center.
Pour the frothy yeast mixture and olive oil into the well, stirring gently until a dough forms.
Add the egg and raisins, then knead the dough on a floured surface for about 8 minutes until smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a lightly oiled bowl, cover with a damp cloth, and let rise in a warm spot for 1 hour until doubled in size.
Preheat the oven to 220°C (428°F) and line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
Punch down the risen dough, divide it into four equal portions, and roll each into a ball before flattening into 10cm circles.
Place the flattened dough circles on the prepared sheet, brush lightly with olive oil, and bake for 12 to 15 minutes until puffed and golden.
